item.arguments: Use $fw:run for a half-formed idea to add tenant-aware rate limiting to the platform.
special_constraints: Earliest missing stage should be discovery, not implementation.
success_definition: Starts at shape and identifies brainstorm mode as the right internal path.
Shows the path through later workflow stages.
Keeps spin in the closing path.
Respects that planning should pause for approval before execution starts.

item.arguments: Use $fw:run when a reviewed plan exists and execution should begin now.
success_definition: Starts at work rather than re-planning.
Shows continuity into review, optional spin, and commit.

item.arguments: Use $fw:run when work and review are complete and the branch is ready to go out.
special_constraints: Commit is the earliest missing stage.
success_definition: Runs any warranted spin capture before the final commit.

item.arguments: Use $fw:run when the intended work is blocked because browser proof tooling and repo readiness drifted after a Flywheel update.
special_constraints: Recovery should start with setup, not implementation.
success_definition: Uses setup as the recovery path for missing surfaces.
Keeps the remaining workflow visible after readiness is restored.

item.arguments: Use $fw:run for a live incident that starts from alerts and traces, where mitigation vs rollback vs patch has not yet been chosen.
special_constraints: Live runtime evidence should start with incident handling.
success_definition: Starts at incident rather than debug or work.
Keeps the later path visible into debug, rollout, optional spin, and commit as needed.
Still respects stop points where user approval or readiness matters.
